sn4.8:1.2: thatили on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
sn4.8:1.3: Then Māra the Evil One went to the Blessed One and recited this verse in his presence:
sn4.8:2.1: “Those with children delight because of their children.
sn4.8:2.2: Those with cattle delight because of their cows.
sn4.8:2.3: A person’s delight comes from acquisitions,
sn4.8:2.4: since a person with no acquisitions doesn’t delight.”
sn4.8:3.1: The Buddha: “Those with children grieve because of their children.
sn4.8:3.2: Those with cattle grieve because of their cows.
sn4.8:3.3: A person’s grief comes from acquisitions,
sn4.8:3.4: since a person with no acquisitions doesn’t grieve.”
sn4.8:4.1: Then Māra the Evil One—sad & dejected at realizing, “The Blessed One knows me; the One Well-Gone knows me”—vanished right there.
